comment_text.snp1.1:2.3: In SN 35.241, the “near shore” is the six interior senses and the “far shore” is the six exterior senses.
comment_text.snp1.1:7.1: The commentary interprets vitakka as “wrong thoughts”, but the identical line at Ud 6.7:4.1 clearly refers to jhāna. The commentary also reads vidhūpitā as “burning up”, but vidhūpa refers to clearing the mist, smoke, or incense.
comment_text.snp1.1:9.2: Bodhi, Norman, and Ṭhānissaro all render vitatha as “unreal”. This seems overly idealistic for early Buddhism, so I take the prefix vi in the sense of “distorts”, “twists”.

translation_text.snp1.1:1.1: When anger surges, they drive it out,
translation_text.snp1.1:1.2: as with medicine a snake’s spreading venom.
translation_text.snp1.1:1.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:1.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:2.2: like a lotus plucked flower and stalk.
translation_text.snp1.1:2.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:2.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:3.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:3.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:4.2: as a fragile bridge of reeds by a great flood.
translation_text.snp1.1:4.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:4.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:5.1: In future lives they find no worth,
translation_text.snp1.1:5.2: as an inspector of fig trees finds no flower.
translation_text.snp1.1:5.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:5.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:6.2: passed beyond any kind of existence.
translation_text.snp1.1:6.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:6.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:7.1: Their mental vibrations are cleared away,
translation_text.snp1.1:7.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:7.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:8.1: They have not run too far nor run back,
translation_text.snp1.1:8.2: but have gone beyond all this proliferation.
translation_text.snp1.1:8.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:8.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:9.1: They have not run too far nor run back,
translation_text.snp1.1:9.2: for they know that nothing in the world <j>is what it seems.
translation_text.snp1.1:9.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:9.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:10.1: They have not run too far nor run back,
translation_text.snp1.1:10.2: knowing nothing is what it seems, free of greed.
translation_text.snp1.1:10.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:10.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:11.1: They have not run too far nor run back,
translation_text.snp1.1:11.2: knowing nothing is what it seems, free of lust.
translation_text.snp1.1:11.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:11.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:12.1: They have not run too far nor run back,
translation_text.snp1.1:12.2: knowing nothing is what it seems, free of hate.
translation_text.snp1.1:12.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:12.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:13.1: They have not run too far nor run back,
translation_text.snp1.1:13.2: knowing nothing is what it seems, free of delusion.
translation_text.snp1.1:13.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:13.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:14.1: They have no underlying tendencies at all,
translation_text.snp1.1:14.2: and are rid of unskillful roots,
translation_text.snp1.1:14.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:14.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:15.1: They have nothing born of distress at all
translation_text.snp1.1:15.2: to make them to return to the near shore.
translation_text.snp1.1:15.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:15.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:16.1: No snarl grows in them at alll,
translation_text.snp1.1:16.2: that would shackle them to a new life.
translation_text.snp1.1:16.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:16.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.
translation_text.snp1.1:17.1: They’ve given up the five hindrances,
translation_text.snp1.1:17.2: untroubled, rid of indecision, free of thorns.
translation_text.snp1.1:17.3: Such a mendicant sheds the near shore and the far,
translation_text.snp1.1:17.4: as a serpent its old worn-out skin.

variant_text.snp1.1:1.4: jiṇṇamivattacaṁ → jiṇṇamiva tacaṁ (bj, sya-all, km, pts-vp-pli1); jiṇṇamivā tacaṁ (?)

reference_text.snp1.1:3.1: cck25.264, ms18Sn_4, sya25.325, vnp3, vns3, vri48.88
reference_text.snp1.1:4.1: csp1ed18.280, csp2ed18.280, mc0.336, ms18Sn_5, vnp4, vns4
